title: The Once-In-A-Lifetime Crash No One's Ready For (Worse Than 2008?)
description:
In this video, I talk honestly about what the AI explosion means for jobs, and why some roles are far more at risk than others. Drawing from conversations I’ve had inside Silicon Valley boardrooms, I’ll walk you through how to think about what’s coming and how to stay ahead of it.

If you want to future-proof your career instead of reacting too late, this video will help you think more clearly about your next move.
transcript:
The AI bubble is about to burst. This is
what many in Silicon Valley are saying.
Maybe true, maybe not. But here's what
is true. Last year, $650
billion went into AI [music] and 1.1
million jobs disappeared at the same
time. I've seen cycles like this before.
real estate, crypto, and I get to sit in
boardrooms deciding which AI projects

which jobs are going to be at stake. The
story is always the same. The smart
ones, you know, the [music] top 1%

want to share that exact playbook
[music] so you can stay ahead, become

your ankles and think, "Well, I'm dry.
Looks perfectly safe."
>> [music]
>> But a thousand miles away, sensors show
a wall of water racing toward shore at
500 m an hour. That's what happens in
every single tsunami. [music] And that

to know. How to recognize if you are
standing in the flood zone right now. I

"Well, we're not ready for prime time

and that's the Flood Zone reality. Great

how do you know if your company is in
the flood zone? You can apply a
framework I call rail. R A I L. Just
four questions, takes about 50 seconds.
R is revenue. Is your AI used by real
paying customers? [music] And if the

eventually we'll get there, that's a red

[music] no revenue, no boat. A is

So figure out where the friction is and
how can AI remove [music] it. This is
your efficiency play. If you can't show
real improvement in speed and efficiency
from AI, it's a flood zone. I stands
[music] for inmarket. So it's possible
that you might be working on an internal

it in the hands of your real users? Is
it gathering real data? If it's just
sitting on a shelf in a test

It's a hobby. And finally, L is
learning. Are you learning from real
customer use? This fourth one is a
dealbreaker because one week of watching

more than 6 months of internal testing.
Also, the pace of AI is so feverish

learning, you're already [music] losing.
And I've spoken about companies like
cursor before. They are an AI based

every single day. That is [music] the
velocity of innovation that we all have

[music] How do you get out of the flood

number four is about avoiding the

[music]

It doesn't matter if you're a software

The pattern is going to be the same.

[music] audit your job. How much of your

Scripted work is [music] like the

Strategic work is about solving problems

Step number two, fire yourself from the

that time in developing your human edge

[music]
stare out the window, take long walks,

That is where you can connect the dots

[music] you. Not without the right

The three Rs, rigor, relationship,
[music] and resilience. There are

They have [music] come and gone. But

They understood their domain and the

Rigor, deep understanding of your

your data, not surface level knowledge,

And the final R is resilience. If you

[music]
and adaptability

panicking. It screams into the ocean.
Why aren't you afraid? Look ahead. We're
going to crash. It's over. And the ocean

"You still don't understand, do you?"
And says, "You [music] think you're a
wave. You're water. You are the ocean.
Most external things in our lives are

that's the water. The form [music]
